run_summary: Screen 2 is holding MOD through its earnings print (T-3, print May 26). Three new flags arrive: GTLB (UNDERDONE, conf-3, T-6) clears the bar cleanly on reaffirmed guide + margin-positive RIF; VSCO (OVERDONE, conf-3) and MDB (OVERDONE, conf-3) are structurally long-only ineligible despite sound pre-earnings reads. MOD position remains intact and is held to the print.

entries.name: Victoria's Secret & Co.
entries.thesis: Filings show a quantified, ongoing cost headwind ($85M FY25 op income hit from tariffs) layered on top of a still-shrinking store fleet and a leadership team that turned over its CEO, CFO/COO, and CMO within ~12 months. Q1 is structurally the weakest quarter for an intimates retailer and is the first one fully exposed to the new Section 122 10% global tariff layered over a leadership team that just took the seat. The combination raises the probability of either a Q1 miss or — more likely — a cautious FY26 guide that the market is under-pricing.
entries.what_kills: Mitigation actions (pricing, sourcing shifts) already in place show up as in-line gross margin; management quantifies a smaller-than-expected FY26 tariff impact; Q1 comps positive on a clean basis.
entries.reasoning: OVERDONE — long-only book has no way to express this. Classification is OVERDONE (confidence 3). Screen 2's paper portfolio is long-only; it has no short or synthetic mechanic to express a pre-print read that the Street is too bullish. The flag itself is sound (10-K quantifies $85M FY25 tariff hit, flags lead-time on mitigation, new leadership team in weaker Q1 seasonal), but structure bars execution.

entries.name: MongoDB
entries.thesis: Filings show Atlas consumption deceleration in the existing base (the engine of the model), and the company is simultaneously absorbing a CEO transition and the announced departure of the head of Field Operations — a combination that typically produces conservative first-quarter-under-new-CEO guidance and elevated execution risk on enterprise expansion. Market sentiment around MDB has historically swung on Atlas commentary; a soft or 'reset' guide is plausible and not fully priced.
entries.what_kills: Atlas re-acceleration commentary, raised FY guide, evidence Voyage AI / AI workload monetization is offsetting core deceleration, or net expansion rate stable/up versus prior quarter.
entries.reasoning: OVERDONE — long-only book has no way to express this. Classification is OVERDONE (confidence 3). Screen 2's paper portfolio is long-only. Despite sound filings evidence (Q3 10-Q explicitly discloses Atlas existing-application deceleration; simultaneous CEO and Field Ops head turnover heading into Q1 under new leadership), the read is that the market is under-discounting downside risk, which cannot be expressed as a long position in this structure.
